label: Weaviate
description: Connect to a Weaviate vector database cluster to store, search, and retrieve high-dimensional embeddings.

form.title: Connect Weaviate
form.subtitle: Paste your cluster URL and API key to start querying your Weaviate vector database.

methods.label: Cluster URL + API Key
methods.description: Paste your Weaviate cluster endpoint and API key. Works for both Weaviate Cloud Services (WCS) managed clusters and self-hosted instances with API key auth enabled.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with Cluster URL and API Key

### Weaviate Cloud Services (WCS) — managed cluster

1. Sign in to [Weaviate Cloud](https://console.weaviate.cloud/).
2. Open your cluster from the **Clusters** list.
3. Copy the **Cluster URL** shown on the cluster details page (looks like `https://your-cluster.weaviate.network`).
4. Click **API Keys** in the left sidebar (or the **Details** tab).
5. Copy the **Admin** key or create a new one with the scopes you need.
6. Paste both values into the fields below.

### Self-hosted Weaviate

1. Make sure your Weaviate instance is running with API key authentication enabled. In `docker-compose.yml` or your config, set:
   - `AUTHENTICATION_APIKEY_ENABLED=true`
   - `AUTHENTICATION_APIKEY_ALLOWED_KEYS=your-secret-key`
2. Your cluster URL is the host + port where Weaviate is reachable (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`).
3. Paste the URL and the key you configured into the fields below.

### Notes

- Anonymous (no-auth) clusters: leave the **API Key** field blank if your instance runs without authentication.
- Keys are stored locally in Anton only — never sent to Weaviate's servers except as a Bearer token header on each request.

fields.name: cluster_url
fields.label: Cluster URL
fields.description: The full URL of your Weaviate instance, including protocol. For WCS, copy it from the cluster details page.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Admin or read-only API key for the cluster. Leave blank only if your instance has no authentication enabled.

methods.label: OIDC / OpenID Connect
methods.description: Authenticate with an OIDC provider (e.g. Okta, Azure AD, or WCS built-in) by supplying a client ID and secret. Best for teams with an identity provider already managing access.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OIDC

Weaviate supports OIDC authentication as an alternative to static API keys. You will need an OIDC client registered with your identity provider.

### Weaviate Cloud Services built-in OIDC

1. Sign in to [Weaviate Cloud](https://console.weaviate.cloud/).
2. Open your cluster → **Details** tab.
3. Under **OIDC configuration**, note the **Token URL** (issuer endpoint).
4. Register a new client application in your identity provider (e.g. Okta, Azure AD, or WCS itself).
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into the fields below.
6. Set the **Token URL** to the issuer's token endpoint, e.g. `https://auth.wcs.api.weaviate.io/auth/realms/SeMI/.well-known/openid-configuration`.

### Self-hosted with external IdP

1. In your Weaviate config, set `AUTHENTICATION_OIDC_ENABLED=true` and point it to your IdP's issuer URL.
2. Create a client in your IdP with the `client_credentials` grant type.
3. Paste the client ID, secret, and token URL below.

### Notes

- The token URL is typically the IdP's `/token` endpoint, not the discovery document URL.
- Tokens are short-lived; Anton will use the client secret to re-request a token when the current one expires.

fields.name: cluster_url
fields.label: Cluster URL
fields.description: The full URL of your Weaviate instance.

fields.name: token_url
fields.label: OIDC Token URL
fields.description: The token endpoint of your identity provider. Find it in your IdP's OIDC discovery document under `token_endpoint`.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The OIDC client ID registered with your identity provider.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The OIDC client secret. Stored locally and used only to request access tokens.
